Pyrus communis, also known as the common pear, is a deciduous tree or shrub species in the family Rosaceae. The health benefits of Pyrus communis include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and sore throat.
